properties.remark: MUD AND ROCK SLIDE REPORTED AT I-80 AND VISTA BLVD. NDOT AND NHP MONITORING THE AREA.
RAGGIO PKWY. CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING OVER ROADWAY.
PRATER WAY BETWEEN GREENBRAE AND SPARKS BLVD IS CLOSED AND GLENDALE BETWEEN MCCARRAN AND DORTHY IS CLOSED DUE TO ROADWAY FLOODING.
ROCK BLVD IS CLOSED BETWEEN I-80 AND HYMER RD. DUE TO ROADWAY FLOODING.
FLOODING REPORTED OVER CA HWY-89 NEAR MCKINNY CREEK IN TAHOMA ON THE WEST SHORE OF LAKE TAHOE.
RENO NHP REPORTED A LEVEE BREAK ALONG THE TRUCKEE CANAL NEAR FARM DISTRICT ROAD AND RICCI ROAD IN FERNLEY. THEY REPORTED THAT APPROXIMATELY 200 HOMES WERE UNDER 3 FEET OF WATER.
PEAK WIND GUST ALONG SIERRA CREST WEST OF LAKE TAHOE.
BRIDGEPORT RAWS
SUSTAINED WINDS 44 MPH.
PEAK GUST WITHIN PREVIOUS HOUR AT BRIDGEPORT RAWS. EL. 6650 FT.
SUSTAINED WINDS 44 MPH.
VISIBILITIES REDUCED TO LESS THAN 1/4M ON PYRAMID HWY AND LA POSADA DR. DUE TO BLOWING DUST.
AT HORSE LAKE RAWS...WITHIN THE PREVIOUS HOUR

PEAK WIND GUST IN GERLACH AT 51 MPH SOMETIME BEFORE 730PM. BLOWING DUST REDUCING VISIBILTY TO 1/4-1/2 MILE AT TIMES FROM 20 SOUTH OF GERLACH TO GERLACH ALONG NV STATE ROAD 447...FROM MILE MARKER 57 BETWEEN 715P-745P.
MEASURED AT FALLON ASOS
OUTFLOW FROM EARLIER STORMS NEAR FALLON
REPORTED GUST OF WIND TO 62 MPH
AT 320 PM PDT WINDS REPORTED AT 40 MPH WITH A GUST TO 60 MPH AT PYRAMID LAKE BOAT RAMP.
A 59-MPH WIND GUST WAS RECORDED BY THE DEAD CAMEL RAWS.
A 60 MPH WIND GUST WAS RECORDED AT THE DRI SENSOR AT RAWHIDE FLATS.
WINDS GUSTED TO APPROXIMATELY 50 MPH BREAKING OFF A FEW TREE BRANCHES IN SUTCLIFFE. A BOAT WAS SUNK ON PYRAMID LAKE DUE TO WHITECAPS PRODUCED BY THE STRONG WINDS. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
STREET CLOSURES IN STEAD DUE TO SEWERS BACKING UP AND RUNOFF.
SEVERAL ROADS IN THE STEAD AREA REMAINED CLOSED DUE TO MUD COVERING THE ROAD. MEDIA REPORTED STREETS...A SKATE PARK AND CRAWL SPACES OF HOMES WERE FLOODED.
67 MPH WIND GUST MEASURED 3 MILES EAST OF LAHONTAN RESERVOIR.
HAIL ACCUMULATED TO 3-4 INCHES DEEP. ALSO ESTIMATED 70-80 MPH WIND GUST.
RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO REPORTED ON THE GROUND WITH SEVERE THUNDERSTORM 5 MILES WEST OF FALLON.
50-60 MPH WINDS ESTIMATED WEST OF FALLON WITH POWER OUTAGES REPORTED IN AREA. ALSO AREAS OF BLOWING DUST REPORTED.

APPROXIMATELY 0.75 TO 1.50 INCHES OF RAIN FELL FROM 1600L-1700L CAUSING LOCALIZED FLASH FLOODING IN PALOMINO VALLEY,NV REPORTED BY NWS HYDROLOGIST. OVER 1 MILE OF WILCOX RANCH ROAD SOUTH OF BASQUE OVEN ROAD WAS WASHED OUT AND LOTS OF DEBRIS COVERED ROAD. MINOR PROPERTY DAMAGE AS FENCING AND OTHER PROPERTY WASHED AWAY. AREA RESIDENTS REPORTED HAIL 2 TO 3 INCHES DEEP BEFORE IT WAS WASHED AWAY BY HEAVY RAINS PLUS VERY STRONG WINDS WERE REPORTED. FLOODING OCCURRED FROM APPROXIMATELY 1630L TO 1800L.
TORNADO REPORTED ON THE GROUND 5 MILES NORTH OF FALLON BY TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER.
HAIL ACCUMULATING ON GROUND.
HAIL ACCUMULATING WITH HEAVY RAINFALL. LOCALIZED FLOODING REPORTED.
BLOWING DUST WITH REDUCED VISIBILITIES REPORTED IN EAGLEVILLE FROM SEVERE THUNDERSTORM OFF TO THE EAST.
